title: Token zeroed on drop via Zeroizing<String> in VaultBackend
description: Storing token as Zeroizing<String> in VaultBackend is correct and a genuine improvement over vaultrs (which copied the token into a plain String inside VaultClient). A contributor might suggest using a plain String for simplicity; resist: the auth token is a secret and should be zeroed when the backend is dropped, even though OS networking buffers are not zeroized.
